message.content: Fetch the webpage at https://www.erdosproblems.com/602 and return ALL of the text content, including any references, citations, links, and variant descriptions. I need every detail from the page.

input.prompt: Return ALL of the text content from this page exactly as it appears. Include every single detail: the problem number, title, subject tags, problem statement, all descriptions, all variant descriptions, all references, all citations, all links, all notes, and any other text on the page. Do not summarize or omit anything. Reproduce the full content.

content.content:
# Erdős Problem #602 - Full Text Content

**Problem Number & Status:** #602 | OPEN

**Problem Statement:**
"Let $(A_i)$ be a family of sets with $|A_i|=\aleph_0$ for all $i$, such that for any $i\neq j$ we have $|A_i\cap A_j|$ finite and $\neq 1$. Is there a $2$-colouring of $\cup A_i$ such that no $A_i$ is monochromatic?"

**Tags:** combinatorics | set theory

**Source:** [Er87](#cite-Er87-602)

**Description:** A problem of Komjáth. The existence of such a $2$-colouring is sometimes known as Property B.

**Disclaimer:** "The open status of this problem reflects the current belief of the owner of this website. There may be literature on this problem that I am unaware of, which may partially or completely solve the stated problem. Please do your own literature search before expending significant effort on solving this problem. If you find any relevant literature not mentioned here, please add this in a comment."

**Associated Data:**
- View LaTeX source: Available
- View history: Available
- Formalised statement: No
- Comments: 0 on this problem

**Interaction Metrics:**
- Likes this problem: None
- Interested in collaborating: None
- Currently working on this problem: None
- Problem difficulty assessments: None recorded

**Navigation:** Previous problem [#601](/601) | Next problem [#603](/603)

**Citation Format:** T. F. Bloom, Erdős Problem #602, https://www.erdosproblems.com/602

content.text:
Here is the full text content from https://www.erdosproblems.com/602:

---

**Problem #602** | Status: **OPEN**

**Tags:** combinatorics, set theory

**Problem Statement:**

"Let $(A_i)$ be a family of sets with $|A_i|=\aleph_0$ for all $i$, such that for any $i\neq j$ we have $|A_i\cap A_j|$ finite and $\neq 1$. Is there a $2$-colouring of $\cup A_i$ such that no $A_i$ is monochromatic?"

**Source:** [Er87]

**Description:** A problem of Komjath. The existence of such a $2$-colouring is sometimes known as Property B.

**Disclaimer:** "The open status of this problem reflects the current belief of the owner of this website. There may be literature on this problem that I am unaware of, which may partially or completely solve the stated problem. Please do your own literature search before expending significant effort on solving this problem. If you find any relevant literature not mentioned here, please add this in a comment."

**Additional details:**
- Formalised statement: No
- Comments: 0
- View LaTeX source: Available
- View history: Available

**Interaction metrics:**
- Likes: None
- Interested in collaborating: None
- Currently working on this problem: None
- Problem difficulty assessments: None recorded

**Navigation:** Previous problem: #601 | Next problem: #603

**Citation format:** T. F. Bloom, Erdos Problem #602, https://www.erdosproblems.com/602

---

The reference **[Er87]** refers to a 1987 Erdos paper (the specific bibliographic entry was not expanded on the page). The problem is attributed to **Komjath** and concerns **Property B** -- whether a 2-colouring exists for the union of a family of countably infinite sets (with pairwise intersections finite and not equal to 1) such that no set in the family is monochromatic.
